strengths: 15+ years of hands-on fullstack experience, with recent production work in the core stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB — at Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq
Direct AI agent development experience at re:cinq, building MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing and engineering-quality culture: practiced TDD, pairing, CI/CD and trunk-based development, authored test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught testing workshops
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed, and Kotlin/Java exposure is dated (robotics work at Altom, 2016-2018) rather than recent backend service work
Currently in a career gap since October 2025, with the most recent role lasting six months
Substantial portions of career depth are in DLang and niche ecosystems rather than the JVM/Python breadth the role lists

key_factors.factor: AI agent development experience (MCP, agentic tooling)

response.justification: The candidate is a strong match for this role: he comfortably exceeds the 6+ years requirement with over a decade of fullstack delivery, and his three most recent positions map directly onto the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, and Docker/Kubernetes. Critically, he brings verified AI agent development experience (MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks), which the job lists as a required skill and which few senior fullstack candidates can demonstrate. His track record of TDD, CI/CD, code review culture, open-source test tooling, and teaching workshops speaks to exactly the code-quality, collaboration, and process-improvement responsibilities of the role, and polyglot experience across TypeScript, Java, C++, PHP, Swift, and DLang evidences the multi-language proficiency requested. Gaps around Nest.js specifically and recent JVM work appear readily bridgeable given his demonstrated history of picking up new stacks quickly across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems.
